channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Here's what to expect from Friday's release of the April jobs report
item.description: The total picture is one of a labor market that, while undoubtedly cooling, is generally stable and resilient despite a number of challenges.

item.title: OpenAI trial: Mother of Musk's children says he offered Altman a Tesla board seat
item.description: Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who has four children with Musk, testified that the Tesla CEO wanted the AI startup to join his EV company.

item.title: Ryan Cohen's mysterious bank letter backing GameStop's eBay bid reveals a big issue with deal
item.description: The combined company would need to maintain an investment-grade credit profile, GameStop's mysterious financing letter said.

item.title: Options traders love this under-the-radar AI play
item.description: For an $18 billion market-cap company, data-center owner Iren trades like one of the AI giants.

item.title: One defense stock has fallen so far it's attractive for a short-term bounce and longer-term, Jay Woods says
item.description: The lead contractor on the F-35 fighter peaked on March 2, literally the first trading day after the Iran war started.

item.title: While many international brands retreat, McDonald’s is supersizing its China business
item.description: McDonald's plans to have 10,000 locations in China by the end of 2028, and the country is its second largest market.

item.title: Paul Tudor Jones says U.S. is late to regulating AI: 'We should have already done it'
item.description: The U.S. is locked into a heated rivalry with China and a race to lead AI innovation.

item.title: Boeing, Citigroup CEOs set to join Trump on China visit next week
item.description: Trump's initial travel plan to China was delayed by about six weeks at the U.S.' request in light of the Iran war.

item.title: Planet Fitness stock plunges 30% after company slashes guidance, cancels planned price hikes
item.description: Shares of Planet Fitness plunged Thursday after the fitness company slashed its guidance and canceled planned price hikes.

item.title: Shake Shack shares crater 30% after burger chain reports operating loss
item.description: Shake Shack CEO Rob Lynch said winter storms and an increase to its projections for store openings this year weighed on the company's results.

item.title: How the U.S. military and Maersk got a ship to pass through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Maersk CEO Vincent Clerc said the shipping giant had deliberately taken a "very cautious approach" during the Middle East crisis.

item.title: France is fan favorite for the FIFA World Cup — but AI is backing another nation for glory, says BofA
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is expected to add over $40 billion to the global GDP with its most lucrative edition ever.

item.title: Media's TV upfront presentations have become an NFL showcase
item.description: Almost every media company showcasing its programming to advertisers in New York City next week will tout NFL programming as the centerpiece of their offerings.

item.title: These stocks reporting earnings next week have a history of beating expectations
item.description: Companies including Copa Holdings and Nova have historically beaten analysts' earnings estimates and seen their shares rise as a result.

item.title: Healthy Returns: First Ozempic generics in Canada will be a test case for Novo Nordisk
item.description: New generics in Canada likely mark the start of a decline in international revenue of semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo's Ozempic and Wegovy.

item.title: Iran war market jitters offer silver lining for investors
item.description: Market volatility offers investors a good time to reexamine if their stock allocations align well with their risk tolerance.

item.title: Whirlpool says Iran war causing 'recession-level industry decline.' The shares are down 12%
item.description: "War in Iran resulted in recession-level industry decline in the U.S. as consumer confidence collapsed in late February and March," the company said.

item.title: Kalshi traders see odds rising that a U.S.-Iran nuclear deal will be reached by 2027
item.description: It comes after Axios reported on Wednesday that the two countries were nearing a one-page memo to end their war.

item.title: McDonald's CEO says consumer spending could be 'getting a little bit worse'
item.description: Shares of McDonald's have fallen 10% over the last year, hurt by concerns about the broader economy.

item.title: Used car prices fall for the first time this year and EV interest rises as gas prices spike
item.description: Cox Automotive reports affordability remains a key concern for buyers and that's driving increased demand for older vehicles as well as all-electric vehicles.

item.title: Software stocks stage a comeback. Plus, why Boeing's CEO going to China matters
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Best credit cards for recurring bills and utilities
item.description: CNBC Select highlights the five best credit cards for utilities and other recurring bills.

item.title: Peloton stock rises as higher subscription prices help company drive a profitable quarter
item.description: Peloton CEO Peter Stern told CNBC that the company believes raising prices on its subscriptions was a value-driven move.

item.title: This 'win-win' hedge trade is getting popular with traders
item.description: The trade is fairly simple: sell downside protection in semiconductor names where volatility is expensive, and buy downside protection in the S&P 500, where it's relatively cheap.

item.title: Barclays says it's the best buying opportunity in 20 years for these oil stocks
item.description: Oil service providers, such as Halliburton, should gain as commodity prices appear poised to stay high, with or without the Iran war, the bank says.

item.title: Oil prices edge lower as U.S. waits for Iran response to deal proposal
item.description: Oil prices edged lower as investors continue to assess the latest developments in the Middle East.

item.title: Trump Accounts could allow direct stock donations down the road: Reports
item.description: Trump Accounts could allow direct stock donations, according to reports. Here's what that change could mean for investors.

item.title: Maersk shares drop as CEO warns Iran war will have bigger impact in coming months
item.description: The shipping giant said the Iran war had introduced an "additional layer of uncertainty" as it reported first-quarter earnings on Thursday.

item.title: How startup Propy is deploying $100 million to put real estate deals on the blockchain
item.description: Propy, a Miami-based blockchain technology company that launched in 2017, has been trying to modernize the closing process for residential homes.

item.title: Anthropic CEO says 80-fold growth in first quarter explains 'difficulties with compute'
item.description: At Anthropic's developer conference in San Francisco, CEO Dario Amodei said the company is 'working as quickly as possible' to provide additional compute.
